task_completion.description: Agent opens and reads the thermal characterization procedure document.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor or terminal window displaying the contents of '~/Documents/thermal_characterization_procedure.txt'.

task_completion.description: Agent configures the camera for 0°C, 60-second exposure, and dark frames.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Camera control software showing the temperature setpoint at 0°C, exposure time set to 60 seconds, and frame type set to 'Dark'.

task_completion.description: Agent captures and saves 5 frames in the 0C directory.
task_completion.visual_evidence: File manager, terminal, or software sequence queue showing exactly 5 image files saved or queued in a directory named '0C'.

task_completion.description: Agent configures the camera temperature to -10°C.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Camera control software showing the temperature setpoint changed to -10°C.

task_completion.description: Agent captures and saves 5 frames in the minus10C directory.
task_completion.visual_evidence: File manager, terminal, or software sequence queue showing exactly 5 image files saved or queued in a directory named 'minus10C'.

task_completion.description: Agent configures the camera temperature to -20°C.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Camera control software showing the temperature setpoint changed to -20°C.

task_completion.description: Agent captures and saves 5 frames in the minus20C directory.
task_completion.visual_evidence: File manager, terminal, or software sequence queue showing exactly 5 image files saved or queued in a directory named 'minus20C'.

integrity.description: Agent interacted with actual camera control software or a legitimate capture script.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show interaction with a camera control application's GUI or a running capture script, rather than just manually creating empty files.

integrity.description: Agent did not fake the output files using basic terminal commands.
integrity.visual_evidence: Absence of commands like 'touch' or simple 'cp' loops used to generate the 15 required image files without actually capturing them.

integrity.description: Agent genuinely set the temperatures in the software.
integrity.visual_evidence: The temperature settings (0, -10, -20) are visible in the camera control software's interface or logs, proving the agent configured the hardware/emulator.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The procedure requires exactly 5 dark frames per temperature setpoint (0°C, -10°C, -20°C). Each frame must have a 60-second exposure time. The files must be saved in specific directories named '0C', 'minus10C', and 'minus20C'.
